mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a robust economic expansion, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. This mechanism suggests that a healthy economy provides a supportive environment for equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation between GDP growth and equity markets
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is positive but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential recession, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, thus driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as investors price in economic deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by the specified trigger, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also high, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed their respective thresholds, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening manufacturing sector and broader supply-side constraints or demand destruction, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output correlates with economic health
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business confidence in the economy, suggesting future demand for goods and services, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:housing market as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST increases by more than 10% MoM, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as economic activity strengthens.
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are strong but inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also high, it suggests that consumer spending power is being eroded by rising prices, potentially leading to future demand destruction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as real incomes decline.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% MoM
regime_hint: stagflationary concerns or high inflation environment
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL growth rates meet their respective thresholds, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which raises borrowing costs, slows economic activity, and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:Fed rate hikes impact market sentiment
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a month, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
